speakers.Dev: Detailed planner, sends links/prices/lists, warm but measured. Uses 'babe', 'Cas' for Casey.
speakers.Casey: Emotional, exclamation marks, pet names ('babe', 'babyyyy', 'D'). Career change from law to UX design.

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: good morningggg babe ☀️

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: morning Cas 🥰

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I slept SO good last night omg

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: same honestly. that new pillow is life-changing

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: right?! best $60 ever spent

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: haha truth

messages.content: whatcha doing today

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: omg YES. that looked amazing

messages.content: yes!! their rosemary loaf is insane

messages.content: done. I'll swing by around 4

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: you're the best 🥺

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: anything for you babe

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ugh I love you

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: love you more

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: not possible!!

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: lol ok ok we could do this all day

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: haha fine. I'm gonna go shower

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: k. oh hey what time do you want to eat? like 7?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: 7 is perfect! I'll open that bottle of wine from Sarah's party

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ooh the Malbec? yes please

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: you know it babe 🍷

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: this is gonna be a good Sunday

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: the BEST Sunday

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ok heading to the store. text you later

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: have fun!! love youuu

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: love you ❤️

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: hey how was your day

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ugh

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: honestly terrible

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: oh no what happened

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: just like... everything? we had back to back depositions and then two partners dumped briefs on me at 4pm

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: that's brutal. did you eat?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: no 😭 I had like a granola bar at 2

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: Cas. ok I'm ordering thai. green curry?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: you angel. yes please extra spicy

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: on it

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I'm just so burned out D

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: like I used to love this job and now I literally dread every morning

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I know babe. it's been like this for a while now huh

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: yeah. months honestly

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: have you thought about what you want to do about it?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: idk. like I can't just quit obviously. student loans and everything

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: no I know. just saying we can figure something out if you need a change

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: yeah. maybe. idk

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: can we just watch something dumb on Netflix tonight
messages.notes: Casual fact: Netflix as comfort routine

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: absolutely. that new comedy special?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: perfect

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: Thai should be there in 30. come home whenever

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: leaving now. 15 min

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: love you. seriously. thank you

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: CAS

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: BABE

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: LOOK AT THIS

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: what what what!!

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I found flights to Tokyo for $890 round trip on ANA. direct from LAX!!

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: SHUT UP

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I'm dead serious. March dates too which is cherry blossom season 🌸

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: oh my god oh my god oh my god

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: we've been talking about Japan for YEARS

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I know!! the prices are usually like $1400+ so this is insane

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ok wait can we actually afford it tho

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I ran the numbers. flights would be $1780 for both of us. I think we could do 2 weeks for about $5k total including everything
messages.notes: Fact: total budget estimate $5k for 2 weeks

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: hmmm that's a lot but also like... when else are we gonna find $890 flights

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: exactly. and we've got like $3200 in the travel fund already

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: wait we do?? I thought it was like $2k

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: nah I've been adding $200/month since August

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: babyyyy you're incredible

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I try 😌 so is that a yes?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: YES. a million times YES!!

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: let's gooooo 🇯🇵

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ok but I need to request the time off. when exactly in March?

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: the cheapest dates are March 15 to 29. two full weeks

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I'll email my manager tomorrow. omg I'm literally shaking

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: haha same. ok I'm gonna put together a rough itinerary tonight

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: of course you are lol. classic Dev

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: you love my spreadsheets

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I really do 😂

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: alright let me start researching neighborhoods. I'll send you options

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: this is the best day ever!!!

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I am going to scream

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: uh oh. what now

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: Gerald is the WORST human being alive

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: he literally yelled at me in front of the whole team because I missed a comma in a filing

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: a comma?? are you serious

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: A COMMA. like it wasn't even a substantive error!!

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: that's completely unacceptable. has he done this before?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: yeah he's been getting worse honestly. last week he made Rachel cry in a meeting
messages.notes: Fact: Gerald made Rachel cry too

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: is there an HR process for this?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: lol D this is big law. Gerald IS the process. he's a name partner
messages.notes: Fact: Gerald is a name partner

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: that's messed up. I'm sorry babe

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: like I went to law school for 3 years and $180k in debt to get screamed at about commas

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I know. you deserve better than this

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: honestly today made me realize I don't think I even want to be a lawyer anymore

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: wow. ok. like... at all?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: idk. maybe? I've been thinking about it for a while but today just

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: like broke something in me? does that make sense

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: it makes total sense. we can talk about it more when you get home

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: yeah. sorry for the vent

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: never apologize for that. that's literally what I'm here for

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: 🥺 what did I do to deserve you

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: you existed. that's enough

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ok I'm gonna cry again but in a good way this time lol

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I'll have dinner ready when you get home. the leftover ramen
messages.notes: Casual fact: leftover ramen at home

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: perfect. leaving in 20

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: take your time. I love you

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: love you so much D

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ok babe Japan planning time. I made a spreadsheet 📊

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: omg here we go hahaha I love it

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: so I've been researching neighborhoods. for Tokyo I think we should split between Shibuya and Shinjuku

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: what's the difference?

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: Shibuya is more trendy, shopping, nightlife. Shinjuku has the Golden Gai bar district and better transit connections

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: oh I definitely wanna see Golden Gai!! I saw it on TikTok

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: it's on the list. ok so for Kyoto I found this incredible traditional ryokan

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: a what?

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: it's a traditional Japanese inn. tatami floors, futon beds, communal baths. this one is $120/night

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: omg that sounds amazing!! how many nights in Kyoto?

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I was thinking 4 nights Kyoto, 8 nights Tokyo, 2 nights Osaka
messages.notes: Fact: proposed night split by city

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: wait why Osaka?

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: street food capital of Japan. they call it the 'kitchen of Japan'
messages.notes: Fact: Osaka known for street food

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ok you had me at street food

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: lol figured. ok budget breakdown

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: flights: $1780. accommodation: roughly $1500 for 14 nights. food/transport/activities: $1500. total: $4780

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: so under $5k!!

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: yep. and we already have $3200 saved. need $1580 more by March

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: that's totally doable. like $400/month for 4 months

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: exactly. I can cover most of it. you focus on the loan payments

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: D... you don't have to do that

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I want to. this trip is gonna be incredible

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: it really is. I cannot WAIT to see the cherry blossoms 🌸

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: oh also I found a cooking class in Kyoto. traditional kaiseki cuisine. $80 per person

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: YES. book it immediately

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: done. March 18th afternoon slot

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: this is going to be the best trip of our lives

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: agreed. ok let me book the flights before the price goes up

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: DO IT. omg this is real!!

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: it's real babe 🇯🇵✨

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ok so don't judge me

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: never. what's up

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I've been looking at UX design bootcamps
messages.notes: Life event: exploring career pivot to UX design

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: wait really?? that's awesome!

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: yeah I know it sounds random but hear me out

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: so remember how I redesigned all the filing templates at work and everyone loved them?

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: yeah you spent like an entire weekend on that

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: right and it was the most fun I've had at work in MONTHS. like I was in a flow state the whole time

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I mean that tracks. you've always had a great eye for design
messages.notes: Casual fact: Casey has design aptitude

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: so I found this bootcamp called Designlab. it's 6 months, fully online, and they have a UX career track
messages.notes: Fact: Designlab, 6 months, online, UX career track

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: how much does it cost?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ok so this is the scary part. it's $6,500

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: hmm. that's not nothing but it's not crazy for a career change program either

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: yeah and they have payment plans. like $1100/month for 6 months

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: have you looked at reviews? placement rates?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: YES ok so their placement rate is 89% within 6 months of graduating. and the average starting salary for UX designers in LA is like $85k

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: that's actually really solid. and you'd be way happier

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: right?! like I wouldn't be getting screamed at by Gerald lol

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ok so what's holding you back?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I guess just... the fear? like what if I'm not good enough. what if I'm making a huge mistake leaving law

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: Cas, you literally taught yourself Figma in a weekend. you redesigned an entire template system for fun. you're gonna crush this

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ugh ok when you put it like that

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: just think about it. I'll support whatever you decide. zero pressure

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I love you so much

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: love you too. now go do some secret Figma stuff 😂

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ok so I spent all morning looking at kitchen renovation stuff

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: oh god here we go 😂

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: hear me out! the cabinets are literally falling apart

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: no you're right they ARE bad. the one above the stove doesn't even close anymore

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: exactly. so I got some quotes online and I think we could redo the whole kitchen for $15k-$20k

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: that's... a lot of money babe

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: it is but listen. Shaker-style cabinets in white, quartz countertops, new backsplash. total transformation
messages.notes: Fact: proposed materials — Shaker cabinets, quartz, backsplash

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ok quartz is gorgeous. what color were you thinking?

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I like the Calacatta-look quartz. white with grey veining. it's about $55-75 per square foot installed

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: oooh that's the one that looks like marble right?

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: exactly but way more durable. no sealing, no staining

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ok I'm into it

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: for the backsplash I was thinking subway tile. classic, cheap, easy to clean

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ooh what about that herringbone pattern? my friend Lina just did hers and it's stunning
messages.notes: Preference: herringbone pattern, friend Lina reference

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: oh that would look amazing actually. herringbone subway tile. great call

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: see I have good ideas too 😤

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: never doubted it babe. ok so biggest question: can we do this ourselves or hire someone?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: D you literally flooded the bathroom trying to fix the faucet last year
messages.notes: Casual fact: Dev flooded bathroom previously

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ... that was ONE time
messages.notes: Casual fact: flooding was one incident

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: hire someone lol

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: yeah you're probably right. I'll start getting quotes this week

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: also can we do one of those farmhouse sinks? the big deep ones

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: a Belfast sink? yeah those run about $400-600 for a good one

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: yes!! ok I'm actually getting excited about this

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: same. our kitchen is gonna be incredible

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: we're really adulting huh

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: full blown adulting. Japan trip AND a kitchen reno

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: lol we're either really smart or really dumb

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: why not both 😂

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ok I'll put together a full cost breakdown this week

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: my little spreadsheet king 👑

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: you know it 📊

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: babe. I'm doing it. I'm making ramen from scratch

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: wait ACTUAL from scratch?? like the broth and everything??

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: everything. tonkotsu broth. pork bones, the whole deal

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: D that takes like a million hours doesn't it
messages.notes: Context: awareness of long broth time

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: 12 hours for the broth 😅 I started at 6am

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: you absolute madman lol

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: the apartment smells INSANE already. like a ramen shop

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: omg I can't wait to get home

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ok so here's my ingredient list: 4 lbs pork neck bones, dried shiitake, kombu, niboshi, ginger, garlic, whole black peppercorns

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I have no idea what half of those are but I trust you

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: lol niboshi are dried sardines. they add umami depth
messages.notes: Fact: niboshi = dried sardines for umami

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: you're literally a food scientist now

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I also made the tare — that's the seasoning base. soy sauce, mirin, sake, and a little brown sugar

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: babe you should start a food blog honestly
messages.notes: Casual: idea for Dev to food blog

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: haha nah this is just for us. oh I'm also doing chashu pork belly

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: the rolled up braised one?? 🤤

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: yep. 2 lbs of pork belly rolled and tied, braising for 3 hours in soy-mirin

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I am literally drooling at my desk rn

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: haha sorry. or you're welcome? eggs are marinating too. ajitsuke tamago

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ok I need to stop reading these I'm starving 😩

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: come home at 7. it'll be ready

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I will RUN home

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I got noodles from the Japanese market on Sawtelle too. Sun Noodle brand

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: you thought of everything. I love you so much

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: love you. prepare your taste buds 🍜

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: D can we talk about something serious

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: of course. everything ok?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: yeah. well. kind of

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I've been thinking about this nonstop and I think I want to quit the firm
messages.notes: Decision: wants to quit law firm

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ok. tell me more

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: like actually quit. not 'think about it someday' quit. like give my notice quit

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I hear you. what's the timeline you're thinking?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I want to start Designlab in January. so I'd give two weeks notice in mid-December
messages.notes: Fact: Designlab January start, notice mid-December

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ok and financially — walk me through it

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: so my salary right now is $95k. student loan payments are $1400/month. rent is split with you obviously

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: right. and the bootcamp is $6500

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: yeah. so I've got about $12k in savings. that would cover the bootcamp plus like 4 months of loan payments

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: what about picking up some freelance legal work? contract stuff?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I was thinking that actually! there's a legal research platform called LawClerk where you can do freelance work

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: that's smart. so you'd have some income while doing the bootcamp

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: yeah hopefully enough to cover the loans at least

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: and I can cover a bigger share of rent for 6 months. that's fine

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: babe no that's not fair to you

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: Cas. we're a team. this is what teams do

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I'm literally tearing up at work rn lol

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: well don't let Gerald see 😂

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: hahaha omg

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: so you really think I should do this?
messages.notes: Context: seeking partner validation before career pivot

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: 100%. you're miserable at the firm. you light up when you talk about design. the math works out. what's the downside?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: the downside is I'm 28 starting over from scratch

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: you're 28 with a law degree and the courage to chase something that makes you happy. that's not starting over. that's leveling up

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ok I'm full on crying now thanks a lot 😭❤️

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: good tears?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: the BEST tears. ok. I'm doing it. I'm actually doing this
messages.notes: Decision: final commitment to career change

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: let's go baby 🚀

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: hiiiii babe

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: hey beautiful ❤️

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I miss youuu. this work trip is killing me
messages.notes: Casual fact: Casey on a work trip

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I miss you too. house is too quiet without you
messages.notes: Casual fact: Dev home alone, they live together

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: awww 🥺 how many more days

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: you're back Saturday right?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: yes!! Saturday morning. my flight lands at 10:15am

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I'll pick you up. want me to make brunch?
messages.notes: Casual fact: Dev will pick Casey up from airport

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: omg yes please. your eggs benedict?

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: you got it

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: you're the best human on this planet

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: debatable but I'll take it

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: not debatable 😤

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: lol ok ok. hey I love you

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I love you more

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: not possible

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: hahaha we are disgusting

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: proudly 😂

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ok gotta go to this dinner thing. love you goodnight! 💕

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: night babe. sleep well ❤️

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: Cas.

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: it's booked.
messages.notes: Life event: Japan trip fully booked

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: WHAT. EVERYTHING??

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: everything. flights, Airbnb, ryokan. all of it

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: AHHHHH!!!! 😭😭😭

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ok here are the details

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: flights: ANA, LAX → Narita, departing March 15 at 11:30am, arriving March 16 at 3:45pm local time

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: omg it's so real

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: return: March 29, Narita → LAX, departing 5:10pm arriving same day 11:20am (time zone magic)

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I love time zone magic lol

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: Airbnb in Shibuya confirmed. 1-bedroom, walking distance to Shibuya Crossing. $95/night for 8 nights = $760

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: walking distance to Shibuya Crossing?! babe!!

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: yep. the reviews are insane. 4.97 stars, 200+ reviews. the host speaks English too

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: you're unreal. what about Kyoto?

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ryokan Kumo no Ue — 'above the clouds.' 4 nights, $120/night = $480. includes breakfast!
messages.notes: Fact: ryokan name, price, breakfast included

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ryokan Kumo no Ue 🥺 that's so beautiful

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: and Osaka: a hostel-hotel hybrid near Dotonbori for 2 nights. $65/night

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ok so total damage?

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: flights: $1780. accommodation: $1370. we're at $3150 so far with $1850 left for food/transport/activities

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: babe this is so well planned I could cry

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I already bought a Japan Rail Pass too. 14-day pass, $420 for both of us. unlimited bullet trains
messages.notes: Fact: JR Pass $420 for both, 14 days, unlimited shinkansen

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: BULLET TRAINS

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: told you I'd handle the spreadsheet 😌

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: best spreadsheet you've ever made and you've made A LOT

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: 98 days and counting 🇯🇵

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I literally cannot wait. this is going to change our lives

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: agreed. now help me pick restaurants. I have a list of 47
messages.notes: Casual fact: Dev curated 47 restaurant options

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: FORTY SEVEN?! 😂 ok send it

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ok renovation update. I got three contractor quotes back

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ooh ok lay it on me

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: contractor 1: Mike's Remodeling. $22k. 8 weeks. decent reviews

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: oof $22k is over budget

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: yeah. contractor 2: some guy named Steve off Thumbtack. $14k but his reviews are sketchy

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: hard no on sketchy Steve lol

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: lol agreed. contractor 3 though... Marco from Roma Construction

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ok?

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: $18k, 6 week timeline, 4.9 stars on Yelp with 85 reviews. he specializes in kitchen and bath
messages.notes: Fact: Marco's quote $18k, 6 weeks, 4.9 stars, 85 reviews

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: oh I like that. $18k is right in our budget

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: AND he came over yesterday and was super professional. brought a portfolio, references, the whole thing

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: wait he came to the apartment already?

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: yeah I wanted to see how he carried himself. he measured everything, asked about our style preferences

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: aw D you did all that while I was out?

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: of course. he also said the Calacatta quartz we want is a great choice. he works with a supplier that does $62/sqft installed

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: omg that's on the lower end of what you quoted before!

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: exactly. I think Marco is our guy

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I agree. when would he start?

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: he said he could start mid-January. so we'd be done by end of February
messages.notes: Fact: start mid-January, done end of February

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: before the Japan trip!! perfect timing

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: that's what I was thinking. come home to a brand new kitchen AND jet lag lol

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: hahaha living our best life

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ok one more thing. we decided on quartz. do we want the Shaker cabinets in white or navy?
messages.notes: Context: setting up cabinet color decision

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: oh that's tough. can we do white uppers and navy lowers?? I saw that on Pinterest

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ooh two-tone. that's actually trending right now. I love it

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: yesss ok I'm so excited!! tell Marco we're in

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: sending him an email right now. we're locked in babe

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: our kitchen is gonna be SO cute omg

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: cute AND functional. that's the Dev special 😌

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: 😂 love you nerd

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: D

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I did it

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I enrolled in Designlab AND I gave my two weeks notice today
messages.notes: Life event: enrolled in Designlab + gave notice

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: CASEY

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: OH MY GOD

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I am SO proud of you babe

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I'm shaking lol

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: tell me everything. how did it go?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ok so I walked into the managing partner's office this morning. Janet, not Gerald obviously lol
messages.notes: Fact: told Janet (managing partner), not Gerald

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: and I just said it. I said I'm pursuing a career change and my last day will be December 31st
messages.notes: Life event: gave notice, last day Dec 31st

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: how did she react?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: she was actually really nice about it? she said she could tell I'd been unhappy and she wished me well

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: aw that's classy

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: yeah. she even said if I ever wanted to come back the door was open

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: that's great to have as a safety net. and Gerald?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: lol Gerald just said 'noted' and went back to his laptop

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: wow. shocking. what a gem

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: hahaha right?! ANYWAY. Designlab starts January 6th. 6-month program, I'll be done by July
messages.notes: Fact: Designlab dates — Jan 6 start, done by July

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: July!! that's so exciting. what's the curriculum like?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: so first 2 months are foundations — design thinking, user research, wireframing. then 2 months of UI/visual design. then 2 months of prototyping and portfolio building

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: and you get a mentor right?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: yes!! a 1-on-1 industry mentor for the whole program. mine is a senior UX designer at Spotify named Priya
messages.notes: Fact: mentor Priya, senior UX at Spotify

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: a Spotify designer?? that's incredible

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I KNOW. I literally stalked her LinkedIn and her portfolio is insane

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: Cas this is going to be so good. 2026 is YOUR year

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: our year babe. Japan, new kitchen, new career. we're doing it all!! 🚀

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: we really are. I love you so much. I'm so proud of you

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: babyyyy last night was AMAZING

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: you think?? I was so nervous
messages.notes: Casual fact: Dev nervous about hosting

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: D. you cooked a full 5-course dinner for 8 people and everyone was raving about it

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ok the ramen course was definitely the highlight

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: are you kidding?? the ramen was incredible but that miso-glazed salmon was otherworldly

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: haha I was worried about that one actually. first time doing the 48-hour miso marinade

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: well it was perfect. Sarah literally asked for the recipe like 3 times
messages.notes: Casual fact: Sarah loved it, asked for recipe

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I know 😂 I'll text it to her later

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: and Ryan said it was better than most restaurants he's been to and he's like a total food snob
messages.notes: Casual fact: Ryan's high praise, Ryan is food snob

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ok that one actually means a lot coming from him

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: you should be proud of yourself babe. seriously. a year ago you could barely boil pasta
messages.notes: Temporal: Dev's cooking growth over a year

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: hey that's not entirely... ok yeah that's fair lol
messages.notes: Casual fact: Dev admits prior cooking inability

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: the fact that you went from that to hosting an 8-person dinner party with homemade ramen broth is actually insane

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I guess I found my thing. like how you found design
messages.notes: Context: parallel to Casey's career pivot

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: exactly!! we're both becoming the best versions of ourselves 🥺

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ok don't make me cry at 10am

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: haha too late! ok let's talk Japan. 77 DAYS

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I literally have a countdown widget on my phone
messages.notes: Casual fact: Dev has countdown widget

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: of course you do 😂 ok so what do we still need to do

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ok checklist: 1) get yen exchanged, 2) buy a pocket wifi, 3) figure out the cooking class outfits, 4) Casey needs a new suitcase

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: wait why do I need a new suitcase
messages.notes: Context: setting up broken suitcase reveal

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: babe your suitcase wheel literally fell off on the Portland trip
messages.notes: Casual fact: broken suitcase from Portland

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: oh yeah lol ok fair

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: I found a good one on Amazon. Away carry-on, $275 but there's a 20% off code right now

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ooh Away luggage is so cute. get the green one!

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: the sage green? done
messages.notes: Casual fact: ordered sage green suitcase

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: yesss. ok also I wanted to talk about something

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: what's up?

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I just want to say... this year has been the most transformative year of my life

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: like I went from crying in the office bathroom to enrolling in a design bootcamp. I booked a trip to JAPAN. we're renovating our kitchen

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: Cas...

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: and none of it would have happened without you believing in me when I didn't believe in myself

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: ok NOW I'm crying at 10am

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: GOOD haha

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: you did this though. all I did was hold the flashlight. you walked through the door

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: ugh stop being poetic it's making it worse 😭

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: haha I love you. 2026 is going to be our best year yet

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: new kitchen in February, Japan in March, I finish Designlab in July... it's all happening

messages.speaker: Casey
messages.content: I love you so much D. you're my favorite person in the whole entire world

messages.speaker: Dev
messages.content: you're mine too Cas. always. ❤️
